description_long: This object is a classic portable boombox radio receiver, featuring a rectangular body with two large circular speaker grilles on either side of a central control panel. The front panel includes multiple dials, buttons, and switches, as well as a cassette tape deck and a frequency display window. The top of the device has a handle for carrying, and the back is mostly flat with a battery compartment and screws for access. The exterior is primarily made of molded plastic with metallic and mesh accents on the speaker grilles. The color scheme is predominantly light gray with black speaker covers and control details. This radio receiver is designed for easy transport and use in various urban environments, such as parks, sidewalks, or garages.
description: A classic portable boombox radio receiver with dual large speakers, a central control panel, and a carrying handle, finished in light gray and black.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large black speaker grilles, central control panel with dials, buttons, cassette deck, and frequency display.
description_view_1: Left side view showing the depth of the device and the protruding speaker grille.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a flat back panel with a battery compartment, screws, and a carrying handle.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the depth and the edge of the speaker grille.
